tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Asparagus is a tender green stem vegetable known for its unique taste.
  • It is commonly used in salads, soups, and grilled dishes.
  • It has a slightly earthy and nutty flavor.
  • Highly nutritious and low in calories.
  • Popular in Western cuisine.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Bamboo shoots are young edible stems of bamboo plants.
  • They are widely used in Asian cuisine.
  • They have a crunchy texture.
  • Used in stir-fries and soups.
  • Must be properly cooked before eating.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Banana stem is the edible inner core of the banana plant.
  • It is widely used in South Indian cuisine.
  • It has a crunchy texture.
  • Used in salads and curries.
  • Known for its health benefits.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Celery is a crunchy green stem vegetable widely used in salads.
  • It has a mild, slightly salty flavor.
  • Used raw or cooked.
  • Popular in Western cuisine.
  • Low in calories.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Fennel is a bulb-like stem vegetable with a mild licorice flavor.
  • Used in salads and cooked dishes.
  • It has a crisp texture.
  • Popular in Mediterranean cuisine.
  • Also used as a spice.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Kohlrabi is a swollen stem vegetable with a mild sweet taste.
  • It is crunchy and juicy.
  • Used in salads and curries.
  • Popular in European cuisine.
  • Highly nutritious.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Lotus stem is the edible stem of the lotus plant.
  • It has a crunchy texture and mild taste.
  • Used in Asian dishes.
  • It has a unique hole pattern.
  • Highly nutritious.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Rhubarb is a red stalk vegetable used mainly in desserts.
  • It has a sour taste.
  • Used in pies and jams.
  • Only the stem is edible.
  • Popular in Western cuisine.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Sugarcane is a tall grass with sweet juicy stems.
  • Used to produce sugar and juice.
  • It is chewed for its sweetness.
  • Widely grown in tropical regions.
  • Important agricultural crop.
